Here's what changed:

## `[REDACTED]` filtering
- **Cursor parser** skips user/assistant messages that are only `[REDACTED]`
- **Global timeline** (`timeline.js`) filters redacted messages for all agents
- **mission-control** client also filters via `filterTimeline()` for display

## Sync no longer “reloads” the page
- **Auto-poll** only fetches memory/dashboard — **no sync** on every tick (12s interval)
- **Manual Sync** button runs full sync
- State updates use fingerprints so the UI doesn’t flash/reset when nothing changed
- **basic_frontend** got the same fix (poll without sync)

## UI moved to **mission-control** (Next.js)
Primary dashboard is now `mission-control/`, not `basic_frontend/`:

| Tab | What it shows (backend-backed) |
|-----|--------------------------------|
| **Activity** | ChatGPT-style timeline — left sidebar jump list per agent + thread segments |
| **Code edits** | Expandable diff cards with agent logos |
| **Memory** | `relay_context.md` + IR files from dashboard API |
| **Settings** | Project path, API key, stats |

Also:
- Agent logos on timeline, edits, sidebar
- Removed fake sample artifacts/tasks
- `RelayContext` wired to `/api/projects` + dashboard API
- Header **Sync** button + live stats

## How to run

```bash
# Terminal 1 — Relay API
relay serve

# Terminal 2 — Dashboard UI
cd mission-control
npm run dev
```

Open **http://localhost:6374** (UI) — it talks to Relay at **http://localhost:3001**.

After changing parsers, run **Sync** once (or `relay refresh .`) so existing `[REDACTED]` entries drop out of `memory.json`.

+# Relay — Quick Start
+
+One install gives you the CLI, agent hooks, `.relay/` scaffolding, and Mission Control UI.
+
+---
+
+## 1. Install in your project
+
+```bash
+cd your-project
+npx relay-os init
+```
+
+**`relay init` installs everything:**
+
+| What | Where |
+|------|--------|
+| `.relay/` IR markdown + memory | `.relay/project.md`, `current_task.md`, `decisions.md`, `failures.md` |
+| Agent bootstrap | `.relay/AGENT_BOOTSTRAP.md`, `RELAY.md` |
+| Stop hooks (all agents) | `.cursor/hooks.json`, `.claude/settings.json`, `.codex/hooks.json`, `.github/hooks/`, `.agents/hooks.json` |
+| Patched instructions | `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `.cursorrules` |
+| relay-sync skill | `.cursor/skills/relay-sync/` |
+| Project registry + API key | `~/.relay-os/projects.json`, `.relay/project.json` |
+
+Save the API key printed at init — Mission Control and MCP use it.
+
+---
+
+## 2. Run Relay
+
+```bash
+npx relay-os serve          # API :3001 + Mission Control :6374
+npx relay-os watch .        # background transcript sync
+```
+
+Open Mission Control at **http://localhost:6374** (printed by `relay init` / `relay serve`).
+
+---
+
+## 3. Work in any agent
+
+Stop hooks run after each turn: `sync` → `compile_brief.md` → **you (the agent) update IR** → `relay context .`
+
+| Agent | Hook config |
+|-------|-------------|
+| Cursor | `.cursor/hooks.json` |
+| Claude Code | `.claude/settings.json` |
+| Codex | `.codex/hooks.json` |
+| Copilot CLI | `.github/hooks/relay-os.json` |
+| Antigravity | `.agents/hooks.json` |
+
+Disable hooks: `.relay/config.json` → `"autoAgentUpdate": false`
+
+---
+
+## 4. Pseudo-commands (in agent chat)
+
+Not native slash commands — phrases agents recognize from patched instruction files:
+
+| User says | Agent does |
+|-----------|------------|
+| `/relay update` | sync → compile → update IR from `compile_brief.md` → `relay context .` |
+| `/relay context` | Read `.relay/relay_context.md` |
+| `/relay init` | Run `npx relay-os init` if `.relay/` missing |
+
+Terminal equivalent: `relay refresh .` ≈ `/relay update`
+
+---
+
+## 5. Switch agents
+
+1. Finish work in agent A — stop hook or `/relay update` refreshes IR + handoff.
+2. Open the **same folder** in agent B.
+3. Type `/relay context` or let the agent read `.relay/relay_context.md` at session start.
+
+---
+
+## CLI reference
+
+| Command | Purpose |
+|---------|---------|
+| `relay init [path]` | Scaffold `.relay/` + hooks + agent prompts |
+| `relay install [path]` | Re-apply hooks after upgrade |
+| `relay serve` | API + Mission Control UI |
+| `relay serve --api-only` | API only |
+| `relay watch [path]` | Background sync |
+| `relay sync [path]` | Harvest transcripts → `memory.json` |
+| `relay compile [path]` | Write `compile_brief.md` |
+| `relay context [path]` | Generate `relay_context.md` handoff |
+| `relay refresh [path]` | sync + compile + context |
+| `relay mcp` | MCP server for `.relay` files (stdio) |
+| `relay open` | Print UI + API URLs |
+
+---
+
+## Team mode (optional)
+
+Mission Control defaults to **local mode** when started via `relay serve` (no GitHub login).
+
+For team features (GitHub OAuth, shared workspaces), run Mission Control manually with `.env`:
+
+```bash
+cd mission-control
+cp .env.example .env   # set GITHUB_ID, GITHUB_SECRET, MONGODB_URI, NEXTAUTH_SECRET
+npm run dev
+```
+
+Unset `RELAY_LOCAL` / `NEXT_PUBLIC_RELAY_LOCAL` for auth.
+

--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -1,1 +1,234 @@
-
+# Relay
+
+**One project brain. Any coding agent.**
+
+Relay syncs Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot, Codex, and Antigravity into one timeline, one `.relay/` markdown brain, and one handoff file — so you don't re-explain the project every time you switch tools.
+
+Installing `relay-os` gives you the **CLI**, **agent hooks**, **instruction patches**, **relay-sync skill**, **`.relay/` scaffolding**, and **Mission Control UI** — all from one npm package.
+
+---
+
+## Quick start
+
+```bash
+cd your-project
+npx relay-os init          # once — .relay/, hooks, agent prompts, API key
+npx relay-os serve         # Mission Control :6374 + API :3001
+npx relay-os watch .       # keep running — syncs all agents in background
+```
+
+Work in any agent. Stop hooks (or `/relay update`) prompt the **session agent** to update `.relay/*.md` after each turn. Switch agents → read `.relay/relay_context.md` or type `/relay context`.
+
+---
+
+## Install
+
+| Method | Command |
+|--------|---------|
+| **npm** | `npx relay-os init` |
+| **This repo (dev)** | `npm link` in OrbitOS, then `relay init` in your project |
+| **GitHub** | `npx github:AspiringPianist/OrbitOS init` |
+
+Requires **Node.js 18+**. First `relay serve` installs Mission Control UI dependencies automatically.
+
+---
+
+## What `relay init` installs
+
+Everything is **project-local** (not your home directory), except the project registry at `~/.relay-os/projects.json`.
+
+```text
+your-project/
+├── .relay/
+│   ├── AGENT_BOOTSTRAP.md      ← agents read every session
+│   ├── relay_context.md        ← handoff between agents
+│   ├── compile_brief.md        ← input for IR updates
+│   ├── project.md              ← you + agents maintain
+│   ├── current_task.md
+│   ├── decisions.md
+│   ├── failures.md
+│   ├── memory.json             ← unified timeline (sync)
+│   ├── project.json            ← dashboard URL + API key
+│   └── hooks/                  ← stop-hook scripts
+├── RELAY.md                    ← pointer for any agent
+├── CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md        ← patched with Relay blocks
+├── .github/copilot-instructions.md
+├── .cursorrules
+├── .cursor/hooks.json          ← Cursor stop hook
+├── .cursor/skills/relay-sync/
+├── .claude/settings.json
+├── .codex/hooks.json
+└── .agents/hooks.json
+```
+
+---
+
+## Commands
+
+Run from your **project root** (path defaults to `.`).
+
+| Command | What it does |
+|---------|----------------|
+| `relay init [path]` | Create `.relay/`, patch agent files, install stop hooks, register project |
+| `relay install [path]` | Re-apply hooks + bootstrap (after upgrade) |
+| `relay serve [--port 3001]` | **Mission Control UI** (:6374) + **Relay API** (:3001) |
+| `relay serve --api-only` | API only, no Next.js UI |
+| `relay watch [path]` | Background sync — keeps `memory.json` + `compile_brief.md` fresh |
+| `relay sync [path]` | Harvest agent transcripts → `memory.json` |
+| `relay compile [path]` | Write `compile_brief.md` (input for agent IR update) |
+| `relay context [path]` | Generate `relay_context.md` handoff |
+| `relay refresh [path]` | `sync` + `compile` + `context` in one shot |
+| `relay mcp` | MCP server for `.relay` file access (stdio) |
+| `relay open` | Print Mission Control + API URLs |
+| `relay skill [path]` | Same as `relay install` |
+
+**Examples:**
+```bash
+relay init .
+relay serve
+relay watch .
+relay refresh .
+relay context . --print
+```
+
+**Flags:** `--help` · `--print` (with `context`) · `--skip-ir` (with `refresh`) · `--api-only` (with `serve`) · `--ui-port 6374`
+
+---
+
+## Mission Control UI
+
+Started automatically by `relay serve`:
+
+| Service | URL |
+|---------|-----|
+| **Mission Control** | http://localhost:6374 |
+| **Relay API** | http://localhost:3001/api/health |
+
+Features: multi-project dashboard, activity timeline (all agents), IR markdown editor, per-project API keys.
+
+Local mode (default via `relay serve`) skips GitHub login. For team mode with GitHub OAuth + MongoDB, see [docs/QUICKSTART.md](docs/QUICKSTART.md).
+
+---
+
+## How updates work
+
+| Layer | Who | When |
+|-------|-----|------|
+| **Sync** | Relay CLI | `watch`, stop hook, or `relay sync` |
+| **IR markdown** | **Session agent** (you / the coding agent) | Stop hook after each turn, or `/relay update` |
+| **Handoff** | Relay CLI | `relay context` (after agent updates IR) |
+
+Stop hooks run `sync` + `compile`, then ask the agent to read `compile_brief.md`, update IR files, and run `relay context .`.
+
+---
+
+## Pseudo-commands (in any agent chat)
+
+Not built-in slash commands — agents recognize these from patched instructions:
+
+| Say this | Agent should |
+|----------|----------------|
+| `/relay update` | Sync, update IR from `compile_brief.md`, regenerate handoff |
+| `/relay context` | Read `.relay/relay_context.md` |
+| `/relay init` | Run `npx relay-os init` if `.relay/` missing |
+
+Terminal equivalent: `relay refresh .` ≈ `/relay update`
+
+---
+
+## Sample workflow: portfolio site across three sessions
+
+**Day 1 — Cursor (layout + hero)**
+
+You ask Cursor to scaffold a portfolio. After each substantive turn, the stop hook runs. You type `/relay update` before closing.
+
+Agent updates `.relay/current_task.md`:
+
+```markdown
+## Now
+- Hero section done (gradient + CTA)
+- Next: projects grid + contact form
+```
+
+`.relay/decisions.md` gets:
+
+```markdown
+- 2026-06-14 — Tailwind v4 utilities only, no component library
+```
+
+**Day 2 — Claude Code (projects grid)**
+
+Open the **same folder** in Claude. At session start it reads `.relay/relay_context.md` (or you type `/relay context`).
+
+Claude implements the grid without re-explaining the stack. Stop hook + `/relay update` append to `decisions.md`:
+
+```markdown
+- 2026-06-14 — Project cards: 3-column on desktop, 1-column mobile
+```
+
+**Day 3 — Copilot CLI (contact form + bugfix)**
+
+Switch to Copilot CLI in the same repo. `/relay context` → sees Tailwind decision + grid work. You fix a form validation bug.
+
+Agent adds to `.relay/failures.md`:
+
+```markdown
+- Contact form: forgot `required` on email — fixed in `Contact.tsx`
+```
+
+**Throughout — Mission Control**
+
+With `relay serve` + `relay watch .` running:
+
+- **Activity** tab shows Cursor → Claude → Copilot messages in one timeline
+- **Memory** tab shows live `project.md`, `decisions.md`, etc.
+- **Settings** tab lets you rename the project and copy the API key
+
+**Switching agents checklist**
+
+1. `/relay update` (or let stop hook finish)
+2. Open same project folder in new tool
+3. `/relay context` or read `.relay/relay_context.md`
+
+---
+
+## Stop hooks
+
+Installed by `relay init` in the **project folder**:
+
+| Agent | Config file |
+|-------|-------------|
+| Cursor | `.cursor/hooks.json` |
+| Claude Code | `.claude/settings.json` |
+| Codex | `.codex/hooks.json` |
+| Copilot CLI | `.github/hooks/relay-os.json` |
+| Antigravity | `.agents/hooks.json` |
+
+Scripts live in `.relay/hooks/`. Disable: `.relay/config.json` → `"autoAgentUpdate": false`
+
+---
+
+## MCP
+
+Expose `.relay` files to agents that support MCP:
+
+```bash
+relay mcp
+```
+
+Configure your agent to run this command; use the API key from `relay init` for authenticated file writes.
+
+---
+
+## More docs
+
+[docs/QUICKSTART.md](docs/QUICKSTART.md)
+
+---
+
+## What Relay is
+
+Git tracks code. Relay tracks **project intelligence** — tasks, decisions, failures, and recent work — in markdown your agents (and git) can read.
+
+Agents natively want different instruction files (`CLAUDE.md`, `.cursorrules`, `copilot-instructions.md`, etc.). Relay patches those automatically and keeps one shared `.relay/` brain in sync.
+

Relay is now packaged as a single npm install that ships the CLI, agent integration, `.relay/` scaffolding, and Mission Control UI.

## What changed

### One npm package ships everything
- **`package.json`** — `mission-control` and `scripts/` added to `files[]`; `postinstall` installs UI deps
- **`.npmignore`** — excludes dev artifacts, examples, landing page, build caches

### `relay serve` launches API + Mission Control
- **`backend/lib/relayUi.js`** — spawns Next.js UI on **:6374** with local mode env vars
- **`bin/relay.js`** — `relay serve` starts both processes; `--api-only` for API-only; `relay open` prints correct URLs
- **`relayProjects.js`** — dashboard/setup URLs point to **:6374** (not the API port)

### Mission Control local mode (no GitHub/MongoDB required)
- **`localMode.ts`** — skips auth when `RELAY_LOCAL=1` (set automatically by `relay serve`)
- **`page.tsx`**, **`login/page.tsx`**, **`onboarding/page.tsx`** — work without login in local mode
- **`auth.ts`**, **`mongodb.ts`** — team mode unchanged; local mode uses JWT-only auth

### Docs + cleanup
- **`README.md`** — full command reference, what `init` installs, sample multi-session workflow (Cursor → Claude → Copilot)
- **`docs/QUICKSTART.md`** — updated install/run flow
- Removed stale files: `example_*.json`, `state_report*.json`, planning docs, agent notes (~19MB of sample JSON alone)

## Daily workflow

```bash
npx relay-os init          # hooks, skills, .relay/, agent prompts, API key
npx relay-os serve         # Mission Control :6374 + API :3001
npx relay-os watch .       # background sync
```

Then work in any agent — use `/relay update` and `/relay context` to hand off between sessions. Mission Control shows the unified timeline and live IR markdown.

watch does refresh also?

Checking what `relay watch` and `relay refresh` each run in the codebase.

[REDACTED]

**No — `watch` is not a full `refresh`.**

| Step | `relay watch` | `relay refresh` |
|------|---------------|-----------------|
| **sync** (transcripts → `memory.json`) | ✅ on start + when transcripts change | ✅ |
| **compile** (`compile_brief.md`) | ✅ | ✅ |
| **context** (`relay_context.md`) | ❌ | ✅ |

`watch` keeps sync + compile fresh in the background. It does **not** regenerate the handoff file.

That last step — updating IR markdown and running `relay context` — is meant to happen via **stop hooks** or when you type **`/relay update`** / run **`relay refresh .`**.

So: **`watch` = sync + compile only**. **`refresh` = sync + compile + context**.

| Area | Done? |

## Dependencies

**Optional: `sqlite3` CLI (system binary, not npm)**

**Optional env vars**
- `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` — smarter `relay compile-ir` (heuristics work without)

**Storage = files only**
- `.relay/` in your project
- `~/.relay-os/projects.json` for the project registry

You type `/relay update`. + +`.relay/current_task.md`: +```markdown +## Now +- Hero done (gradient + CTA) +- Next: projects grid +``` + +**Day 2 — Claude Code** opens the same folder. `/relay context` → implements grid without re-briefing. + +**Day 3 — Copilot CLI** fixes form validation, appends to `.relay/failures.md`. + +With `relay serve` + `relay watch .` running, Mission Control shows all three agents on one timeline. + +**Switch checklist:** `/relay update` → open same folder in new tool → `/relay context` + +--- + +## Dependencies + +| | | +|---|---| +| **Required** | Node.js 18+, npm | +| **Auto-installed** | `express`, `cors` (API) · `next`, `react` (Mission Control) | +| **Not needed** | MongoDB, Redis, Docker, login/OAuth | +| **Optional** | [`sqlite3` CLI](https://sqlite.org/download.html) on PATH — richer Copilot sync via VS Code `state.vscdb` (not an npm package) | +| **Optional LLM keys** | `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` for smarter `relay compile-ir` (heuristics work without) | + +**Storage:** `.relay/` in your project + `~/.relay-os/projects.json` — files only, no DB server. + +**Env vars:** `RELAY_PORT` (3001) · `RELAY_UI_PORT` (6374) · `RELAY_API_KEY` (optional API auth) · `RELAY_SKIP_UI_INSTALL=1` + +--- + +## Stop hooks + +Installed in your **project folder** by `relay init`: + +| Agent | Config | +|-------|--------| +| Cursor | `.cursor/hooks.json` | +| Claude Code | `.claude/settings.json` | +| Codex | `.codex/hooks.json` | +| Copilot CLI | `.github/hooks/relay-os.json` | +| Antigravity | `.agents/hooks.json` | + +Disable: `.relay/config.json` → `"autoAgentUpdate": false` + +--- + +## Docs + +[docs/QUICKSTART.md](docs/QUICKSTART.md) + +--- + +

how to install to npx or npm from github repo

From the GitHub repo **`AspiringPianist/OrbitOS`** (npm package name: **`relay-os`**):

### Run once with npx (no global install)

```bash
cd your-project
npx github:AspiringPianist/OrbitOS init
npx github:AspiringPianist/OrbitOS serve
npx github:AspiringPianist/OrbitOS watch .
```

Pin a branch, tag, or commit:

```bash
npx github:AspiringPianist/OrbitOS#main init
npx github:AspiringPianist/OrbitOS#v0.1.0 init
npx github:AspiringPianist/OrbitOS#abc1234 init
```

### Install globally from GitHub

```bash
npm install -g git+https://github.com/AspiringPianist/OrbitOS.git
relay init
relay serve
```

With branch/tag:

```bash
npm install -g git+https://github.com/AspiringPianist/OrbitOS.git#main
```

### Install in a project (dev dependency)

```bash
npm install --save-dev git+https://github.com/AspiringPianist/OrbitOS.git
npx relay init
```

### Local development (while hacking on the repo)

```bash
git clone https://github.com/AspiringPianist/OrbitOS.git
cd OrbitOS
npm install
npm link

cd /path/to/your-project
relay init
```

**Requires Node.js 18+.** First `relay serve` runs Mission Control’s `npm install` automatically.

Once published to npm, you’d use `npx relay-os init` instead of the `github:` form — same commands, shorter URL.

events.content:
Here’s what changed and the honest answer on agent chat from the UI.

## Implemented

**`relay init` now starts Mission Control in the background** (API + UI). First run may take ~30s while deps install. Skip with:
```bash
relay init --no-serve
```

**Relay brain always visible** — right sidebar **RELAY BRAIN** panel shows Handoff, Current task, Decisions, Failures, Project (accordion). **All IR files** tab in the center still has the full split view.

**Collaborators** — sidebar panel; names saved to `.relay/mission_control.json`.

**Agent chat tab** — pick Cursor / Claude / Copilot / Codex / Antigravity, post team notes, get launch instructions for that agent. Stored in the same `mission_control.json`.

## Can you chat with coding agents from Mission Control without opening the IDE?

**Not in the full sense — and we did not wire that up.**

| Agent | From browser only? |
|-------|-------------------|
| **Cursor, Copilot (VS Code), Antigravity** | No — they need their IDE/editor open with the project folder |
| **Claude Code, Codex, Copilot CLI** | Partially — you could shell out to their CLIs from a backend, but that’s a separate integration (terminal sessions, auth, streaming). Relay doesn’t do that today |

What Mission Control **does** do: sync memory, show IR files, coordinate collaborators, and route “use Cursor for this” with `/relay context` instructions. The **Agent chat** tab is for **team notes + which agent to use**, not a replacement for the agent itself.

To actually code with an agent: open the project in that tool (or its CLI), then `/relay context`.

## After pulling these changes

```bash
relay init          # starts UI in background → http://localhost:6374
# or
relay serve         # foreground if you prefer
```

Add your workspace in the UI if it isn’t auto-linked, then check **RELAY BRAIN** on the right and **Agent chat** in the center tabs.

artifacts.content:
# Goal: Add Simple yet Premium 3D Space Animations to the Landing Page

Integrate an interactive, high-performance 3D canvas-based space animation in the hero/intro section of the landing page. The animation will feature a rotating 3D starfield with mouse parallax and a colorful nebula glow, giving a modern, responsive, and state-of-the-art feel to the landing page while maintaining readability and performance.

## User Review Required

> [!NOTE]
> The implementation uses pure vanilla JS and HTML5 Canvas (WebGL/2D Context optimized for 3D projections) to avoid adding heavy external packages like Three.js, which keeps the page load lightning fast.
> The canvas is placed behind the intro text with `pointer-events: none` and relative positioning so that all text, buttons, and links remain fully accessible and copyable.

## Proposed Changes

We will modify three files: `index.html` to add the canvas container, `styles.css` to position it correctly and ensure text readability, and `script.js` to implement the 3D projection, math, animation loop, mouse parallax, and scroll integration.

---

### [Portfolio Front-end]

#### [MODIFY] [index.html](file:///c:/Users/unnat/Desktop/portfolio/index.html)
- Insert `<canvas id="space-canvas"></canvas>` inside the `<section class="intro">` element.
- Ensure the intro elements (`.wrap`) are positioned correctly so they overlay cleanly on top.

#### [MODIFY] [styles.css](file:///c:/Users/unnat/Desktop/portfolio/styles.css)
- Set `.intro` to `position: relative; overflow: hidden;` so the canvas is constrained.
- Add CSS rules for `#space-canvas` to position it absolutely, span the full intro section, and have a fade-out mask at the bottom (so it transitions smoothly into the rest of the dark site).
- Ensure `.intro .wrap` has a higher `z-index` and position: relative so text remains legible and links interactive.

#### [MODIFY] [script.js](file:///c:/Users/unnat/Desktop/portfolio/script.js)
- Implement a `SpaceStarfield` class or module that:
  - Initializes a 2D/3D projection on a high-DPI canvas.
  - Generates 3D particles (`x`, `y`, `z` coordinates) with a color palette matching the current portfolio theme (golds/yellows, soft white, deep spaces blues/purples).
  - Rotates coordinates in 3D using pitch/yaw rotation matrices.
  - Automatically handles canvas resizing.
  - Connects mouse movements to a smooth, inertia-based camera parallax (shifting the yaw/pitch of the camera).
  - Connects scroll behavior to push/pull the stars (changing warp speed or camera depth).
  - Pauses execution using `IntersectionObserver` when the intro section is scrolled out of view to optimize CPU/GPU usage.

## Verification Plan

### Automated/Lint Checks
- We will double check for syntax errors or typos.

### Manual Verification
- Verify that the canvas adapts perfectly to desktop and mobile screens.
- Verify that all existing interactive elements (links, filters, resume download) inside and outside the hero section continue to work perfectly and are not blocked by the canvas overlay.
- Test performance by checking frame rates and CPU usage during scrolling/hovering.

## Changes Made

### 1. HTML Container
- Added a `<canvas id="space-canvas"></canvas>` element to [index.html](file:///c:/Users/unnat/Desktop/portfolio/index.html#L43-L45) directly inside the `.intro` (hero) section.

- Updated [styles.css](file:///c:/Users/unnat/Desktop/portfolio/styles.css#L137-L159) to:
  - Configure `.intro` with relative positioning and hide overflow to confine the canvas container.
  - Position `#space-canvas` absolutely to cover the intro area.
  - Set a `mask-image` linear gradient fade at the bottom of the canvas so it blends seamlessly into the dark page background.
  - Render the `.intro .wrap` container on a higher `z-index` layer to keep the text, links, and action buttons accessible and readable.

### 3. JavaScript 3D Canvas Engine
- Implemented the `SpaceStarfield` class in [script.js](file:///c:/Users/unnat/Desktop/portfolio/script.js#L76-L304) containing:
  - **3D Coordinate Generation & Projection**: Rotates random particle positions in 3D using coordinate yaw/pitch transformation matrices.
  - **Dynamic Theme Color Palette**: Generates particles with gold, soft white, and space blue colors to harmonize with the theme.
  - **Interactive Camera Parallax**: Leverages mouse coordinates to shift the camera yaw/pitch, giving responsive 3D depth.
  - **Scroll Acceleration Warp**: Increases star speed and adds light trails when scrolling to create a sci-fi "warp speed" effect.
  - **Performance Optimization**: Automatically pauses loop rendering when the hero section is out of viewport view, keeping CPU/GPU footprint near-zero.
  - **High-DPI Retina Support**: Automatically scales context resolution using `devicePixelRatio`.

## Verification Results

- Verified that the file structure compiles and standard tag placements are properly styled.
- Checked that page elements, scroll states, and interaction boundaries remain responsive and intact.

Here's what changed:

## `[REDACTED]` filtering
- **Cursor parser** skips user/assistant messages that are only `[REDACTED]`
- **Global timeline** (`timeline.js`) filters redacted messages for all agents
- **mission-control** client also filters via `filterTimeline()` for display

## Sync no longer “reloads” the page
- **Auto-poll** only fetches memory/dashboard — **no sync** on every tick (12s interval)
- **Manual Sync** button runs full sync
- State updates use fingerprints so the UI doesn’t flash/reset when nothing changed
- **basic_frontend** got the same fix (poll without sync)

## UI moved to **mission-control** (Next.js)
Primary dashboard is now `mission-control/`, not `basic_frontend/`:

| Tab | What it shows (backend-backed) |
|-----|--------------------------------|
| **Activity** | ChatGPT-style timeline — left sidebar jump list per agent + thread segments |
| **Code edits** | Expandable diff cards with agent logos |
| **Memory** | `relay_context.md` + IR files from dashboard API |
| **Settings** | Project path, API key, stats |

Also:
- Agent logos on timeline, edits, sidebar
- Removed fake sample artifacts/tasks
- `RelayContext` wired to `/api/projects` + dashboard API
- Header **Sync** button + live stats

## How to run

```bash
# Terminal 1 — Relay API
relay serve

# Terminal 2 — Dashboard UI
cd mission-control
npm run dev
```

Open **http://localhost:6374** (UI) — it talks to Relay at **http://localhost:3001**.

+# Relay — Quick Start
+
+One install gives you the CLI, agent hooks, `.relay/` scaffolding, and Mission Control UI.
+
+---
+
+## 1. Install in your project
+
+```bash
+cd your-project
+npx relay-os init
+```
+
+**`relay init` installs everything:**
+
+| What | Where |
+|------|--------|
+| `.relay/` IR markdown + memory | `.relay/project.md`, `current_task.md`, `decisions.md`, `failures.md` |
+| Agent bootstrap | `.relay/AGENT_BOOTSTRAP.md`, `RELAY.md` |
+| Stop hooks (all agents) | `.cursor/hooks.json`, `.claude/settings.json`, `.codex/hooks.json`, `.github/hooks/`, `.agents/hooks.json` |
+| Patched instructions | `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `.cursorrules` |
+| relay-sync skill | `.cursor/skills/relay-sync/` |
+| Project registry + API key | `~/.relay-os/projects.json`, `.relay/project.json` |
+
+Save the API key printed at init — Mission Control and MCP use it.
+
+---
+
+## 2. Run Relay
+
+```bash
+npx relay-os serve          # API :3001 + Mission Control :6374
+npx relay-os watch .        # background transcript sync
+```
+
+Open Mission Control at **http://localhost:6374** (printed by `relay init` / `relay serve`).
+
+---
+
+## 3. Work in any agent
+
+Stop hooks run after each turn: `sync` → `compile_brief.md` → **you (the agent) update IR** → `relay context .`
+
+| Agent | Hook config |
+|-------|-------------|
+| Cursor | `.cursor/hooks.json` |
+| Claude Code | `.claude/settings.json` |
+| Codex | `.codex/hooks.json` |
+| Copilot CLI | `.github/hooks/relay-os.json` |
+| Antigravity | `.agents/hooks.json` |
+
+Disable hooks: `.relay/config.json` → `"autoAgentUpdate": false`
+
+---
+
+## 4. Pseudo-commands (in agent chat)
+
+Not native slash commands — phrases agents recognize from patched instruction files:
+
+| User says | Agent does |
+|-----------|------------|
+| `/relay update` | sync → compile → update IR from `compile_brief.md` → `relay context .` |
+| `/relay context` | Read `.relay/relay_context.md` |
+| `/relay init` | Run `npx relay-os init` if `.relay/` missing |
+
+Terminal equivalent: `relay refresh .` ≈ `/relay update`
+
+---
+
+## 5. Switch agents
+
+1. Finish work in agent A — stop hook or `/relay update` refreshes IR + handoff.
+2. Open the **same folder** in agent B.
+3. Type `/relay context` or let the agent read `.relay/relay_context.md` at session start.
+
+---
+
+## CLI reference
+
+| Command | Purpose |
+|---------|---------|
+| `relay init [path]` | Scaffold `.relay/` + hooks + agent prompts |
+| `relay install [path]` | Re-apply hooks after upgrade |
+| `relay serve` | API + Mission Control UI |
+| `relay serve --api-only` | API only |
+| `relay watch [path]` | Background sync |
+| `relay sync [path]` | Harvest transcripts → `memory.json` |
+| `relay compile [path]` | Write `compile_brief.md` |
+| `relay context [path]` | Generate `relay_context.md` handoff |
+| `relay refresh [path]` | sync + compile + context |
+| `relay mcp` | MCP server for `.relay` files (stdio) |
+| `relay open` | Print UI + API URLs |
+
+---
+
+## Team mode (optional)
+
+Mission Control defaults to **local mode** when started via `relay serve` (no GitHub login).
+
+For team features (GitHub OAuth, shared workspaces), run Mission Control manually with `.env`:
+
+```bash
+cd mission-control
+cp .env.example .env   # set GITHUB_ID, GITHUB_SECRET, MONGODB_URI, NEXTAUTH_SECRET
+npm run dev
+```
+
+Unset `RELAY_LOCAL` / `NEXT_PUBLIC_RELAY_LOCAL` for auth.
+

--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -1,1 +1,234 @@
-
+# Relay
+
+**One project brain. Any coding agent.**
+
+Relay syncs Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot, Codex, and Antigravity into one timeline, one `.relay/` markdown brain, and one handoff file — so you don't re-explain the project every time you switch tools.
+
+Installing `relay-os` gives you the **CLI**, **agent hooks**, **instruction patches**, **relay-sync skill**, **`.relay/` scaffolding**, and **Mission Control UI** — all from one npm package.
+
+---
+
+## Quick start
+
+```bash
+cd your-project
+npx relay-os init          # once — .relay/, hooks, agent prompts, API key
+npx relay-os serve         # Mission Control :6374 + API :3001
+npx relay-os watch .       # keep running — syncs all agents in background
+```
+
+Work in any agent. Stop hooks (or `/relay update`) prompt the **session agent** to update `.relay/*.md` after each turn. Switch agents → read `.relay/relay_context.md` or type `/relay context`.
+
+---
+
+## Install
+
+| Method | Command |
+|--------|---------|
+| **npm** | `npx relay-os init` |
+| **This repo (dev)** | `npm link` in OrbitOS, then `relay init` in your project |
+| **GitHub** | `npx github:AspiringPianist/OrbitOS init` |
+
+Requires **Node.js 18+**. First `relay serve` installs Mission Control UI dependencies automatically.
+
+---
+
+## What `relay init` installs
+
+Everything is **project-local** (not your home directory), except the project registry at `~/.relay-os/projects.json`.
+
+```text
+your-project/
+├── .relay/
+│   ├── AGENT_BOOTSTRAP.md      ← agents read every session
+│   ├── relay_context.md        ← handoff between agents
+│   ├── compile_brief.md        ← input for IR updates
+│   ├── project.md              ← you + agents maintain
+│   ├── current_task.md
+│   ├── decisions.md
+│   ├── failures.md
+│   ├── memory.json             ← unified timeline (sync)
+│   ├── project.json            ← dashboard URL + API key
+│   └── hooks/                  ← stop-hook scripts
+├── RELAY.md                    ← pointer for any agent
+├── CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md        ← patched with Relay blocks
+├── .github/copilot-instructions.md
+├── .cursorrules
+├── .cursor/hooks.json          ← Cursor stop hook
+├── .cursor/skills/relay-sync/
+├── .claude/settings.json
+├── .codex/hooks.json
+└── .agents/hooks.json
+```
+
+---
+
+## Commands
+
+Run from your **project root** (path defaults to `.`).
+
+| Command | What it does |
+|---------|----------------|
+| `relay init [path]` | Create `.relay/`, patch agent files, install stop hooks, register project |
+| `relay install [path]` | Re-apply hooks + bootstrap (after upgrade) |
+| `relay serve [--port 3001]` | **Mission Control UI** (:6374) + **Relay API** (:3001) |
+| `relay serve --api-only` | API only, no Next.js UI |
+| `relay watch [path]` | Background sync — keeps `memory.json` + `compile_brief.md` fresh |
+| `relay sync [path]` | Harvest agent transcripts → `memory.json` |
+| `relay compile [path]` | Write `compile_brief.md` (input for agent IR update) |
+| `relay context [path]` | Generate `relay_context.md` handoff |
+| `relay refresh [path]` | `sync` + `compile` + `context` in one shot |
+| `relay mcp` | MCP server for `.relay` file access (stdio) |
+| `relay open` | Print Mission Control + API URLs |
+| `relay skill [path]` | Same as `relay install` |
+
+**Examples:**
+```bash
+relay init .
+relay serve
+relay watch .
+relay refresh .
+relay context . --print
+```
+
+**Flags:** `--help` · `--print` (with `context`) · `--skip-ir` (with `refresh`) · `--api-only` (with `serve`) · `--ui-port 6374`
+
+---
+
+## Mission Control UI
+
+Started automatically by `relay serve`:
+
+| Service | URL |
+|---------|-----|
+| **Mission Control** | http://localhost:6374 |
+| **Relay API** | http://localhost:3001/api/health |
+
+Features: multi-project dashboard, activity timeline (all agents), IR markdown editor, per-project API keys.
+
+Local mode (default via `relay serve`) skips GitHub login. For team mode with GitHub OAuth + MongoDB, see [docs/QUICKSTART.md](docs/QUICKSTART.md).
+
+---
+
+## How updates work
+
+| Layer | Who | When |
+|-------|-----|------|
+| **Sync** | Relay CLI | `watch`, stop hook, or `relay sync` |
+| **IR markdown** | **Session agent** (you / the coding agent) | Stop hook after each turn, or `/relay update` |
+| **Handoff** | Relay CLI | `relay context` (after agent updates IR) |
+
+Stop hooks run `sync` + `compile`, then ask the agent to read `compile_brief.md`, update IR files, and run `relay context .`.
+
+---
+
+## Pseudo-commands (in any agent chat)
+
+Not built-in slash commands — agents recognize these from patched instructions:
+
+| Say this | Agent should |
+|----------|----------------|
+| `/relay update` | Sync, update IR from `compile_brief.md`, regenerate handoff |
+| `/relay context` | Read `.relay/relay_context.md` |
+| `/relay init` | Run `npx relay-os init` if `.relay/` missing |
+
+Terminal equivalent: `relay refresh .` ≈ `/relay update`
+
+---
+
+## Sample workflow: portfolio site across three sessions
+
+**Day 1 — Cursor (layout + hero)**
+
+You ask Cursor to scaffold a portfolio. After each substantive turn, the stop hook runs. You type `/relay update` before closing.
+
+Agent updates `.relay/current_task.md`:
+
+```markdown
+## Now
+- Hero section done (gradient + CTA)
+- Next: projects grid + contact form
+```
+
+`.relay/decisions.md` gets:
+
+```markdown
+- 2026-06-14 — Tailwind v4 utilities only, no component library
+```
+
+**Day 2 — Claude Code (projects grid)**
+
+Open the **same folder** in Claude. At session start it reads `.relay/relay_context.md` (or you type `/relay context`).
+
+Claude implements the grid without re-explaining the stack. Stop hook + `/relay update` append to `decisions.md`:
+
+```markdown
+- 2026-06-14 — Project cards: 3-column on desktop, 1-column mobile
+```
+
+**Day 3 — Copilot CLI (contact form + bugfix)**
+
+Switch to Copilot CLI in the same repo. `/relay context` → sees Tailwind decision + grid work. You fix a form validation bug.
+
+Agent adds to `.relay/failures.md`:
+
+```markdown
+- Contact form: forgot `required` on email — fixed in `Contact.tsx`
+```
+
+**Throughout — Mission Control**
+
+With `relay serve` + `relay watch .` running:
+
+- **Activity** tab shows Cursor → Claude → Copilot messages in one timeline
+- **Memory** tab shows live `project.md`, `decisions.md`, etc.
+- **Settings** tab lets you rename the project and copy the API key
+
+**Switching agents checklist**
+
+1. `/relay update` (or let stop hook finish)
+2. Open same project folder in new tool
+3. `/relay context` or read `.relay/relay_context.md`
+
+---
+
+## Stop hooks
+
+Installed by `relay init` in the **project folder**:
+
+| Agent | Config file |
+|-------|-------------|
+| Cursor | `.cursor/hooks.json` |
+| Claude Code | `.claude/settings.json` |
+| Codex | `.codex/hooks.json` |
+| Copilot CLI | `.github/hooks/relay-os.json` |
+| Antigravity | `.agents/hooks.json` |
+
+Scripts live in `.relay/hooks/`. Disable: `.relay/config.json` → `"autoAgentUpdate": false`
+
+---
+
+## MCP
+
+Expose `.relay` files to agents that support MCP:
+
+```bash
+relay mcp
+```
+
+Configure your agent to run this command; use the API key from `relay init` for authenticated file writes.
+
+---
+
+## More docs
+
+[docs/QUICKSTART.md](docs/QUICKSTART.md)
+
+---
+
+## What Relay is
+
+Git tracks code. Relay tracks **project intelligence** — tasks, decisions, failures, and recent work — in markdown your agents (and git) can read.
+
+Agents natively want different instruction files (`CLAUDE.md`, `.cursorrules`, `copilot-instructions.md`, etc.). Relay patches those automatically and keeps one shared `.relay/` brain in sync.
+

timeline.content:
Relay is now packaged as a single npm install that ships the CLI, agent integration, `.relay/` scaffolding, and Mission Control UI.

## What changed

### One npm package ships everything
- **`package.json`** — `mission-control` and `scripts/` added to `files[]`; `postinstall` installs UI deps
- **`.npmignore`** — excludes dev artifacts, examples, landing page, build caches
- **`scripts/postinstall.js`** — installs Mission Control dependencies on `npm install`

### `relay serve` launches API + Mission Control
- **`backend/lib/relayUi.js`** — spawns Next.js UI on **:6374** with local mode env vars
- **`bin/relay.js`** — `relay serve` starts both processes; `--api-only` for API-only; `relay open` prints correct URLs
- **`relayProjects.js`** — dashboard/setup URLs point to **:6374** (not the API port)

### Mission Control local mode (no GitHub/MongoDB required)
- **`localMode.ts`** — skips auth when `RELAY_LOCAL=1` (set automatically by `relay serve`)
- **`page.tsx`**, **`login/page.tsx`**, **`onboarding/page.tsx`** — work without login in local mode

watch does refresh also?

Checking what `relay watch` and `relay refresh` each run in the codebase.

[REDACTED]

**No — `watch` is not a full `refresh`.**

| Step | `relay watch` | `relay refresh` |
|------|---------------|-----------------|
| **sync** (transcripts → `memory.json`) | ✅ on start + when transcripts change | ✅ |
| **compile** (`compile_brief.md`) | ✅ | ✅ |
| **context** (`relay_context.md`) | ❌ | ✅ |

`watch` keeps sync + compile fresh in the background. It does **not** regenerate the handoff file.

That last step — updating IR markdown and running `relay context` — is meant to happen via **stop hooks** or when you type **`/relay update`** / run **`relay refresh .`**.

So: **`watch` = sync + compile only**. **`refresh` = sync + compile + context**.

Yes — the packaging and team/MongoDB removal are done. Here’s the full picture:

## Status

| Area | Done? |
|------|-------|
| npm ships CLI + hooks + skills + `.relay/` + Mission Control | ✅ |
| `relay serve` starts API + UI | ✅ |
| Team auth / MongoDB / GitHub OAuth removed | ✅ |

| Stale `mongodb` removed from root lockfile | ✅ |

## Dependencies

**Required**
- **Node.js 18+** and **npm** — that’s it for core Relay

**Installed automatically via npm**
- **Relay API:** `express`, `cors`
- **Mission Control:** `next`, `react`, `react-dom` (via `postinstall` / first `relay serve`)

**Not needed**
- MongoDB, PostgreSQL, Redis, NextAuth, GitHub OAuth — all removed

**Optional: `sqlite3` CLI (system binary, not npm)**
- Used to read VS Code/Copilot `state.vscdb` files for richer Copilot sync
- Relay shells out to `sqlite3` on your PATH; if it’s missing, Copilot sync may be partial but everything else works
- Install: `winget install SQLite.SQLite` (Windows), `brew install sqlite` (macOS), or [sqlite.org/download](https://sqlite.org/download.html)

**Optional env vars**
- `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` — smarter `relay compile-ir` (heuristics work without)
- `RELAY_PORT` (3001), `RELAY_UI_PORT` (6374), `RELAY_API_KEY` (optional API auth)

**Storage = files only**
- `.relay/` in your project
- `~/.relay-os/projects.json` for the project registry

The README now has a full **Dependencies** section with this breakdown. You can clear old vars from `mission-control/.env` (`GITHUB_*`, `MONGODB_URI`, `NEXTAUTH_*`) — they’re unused now.

+ Relay
+ One project brain. Any coding agent. +

+ +

+ + + + +

+ +

+ +--- + +## Why Relay + +Every coding agent wants its own instruction file — `CLAUDE.md`, `.cursorrules`, `copilot-instructions.md`, and so on. Relay patches them automatically and keeps **one shared `.relay/` brain** in sync across: + +| Cursor | Claude Code | GitHub Copilot | Codex | Antigravity | +|:------:|:-----------:|:--------------:|:-----:|:-----------:| + +**One install** gives you the CLI, stop hooks, agent prompts, relay-sync skill, `.relay/` scaffolding, Mission Control UI, and an optional MCP server. + +--- + +## Quick start + +```bash +cd your-project +npx relay-os init # .relay/, hooks, prompts, API key +npx relay-os serve # Mission Control → :6374 · API → :3001 +npx relay-os watch . # background sync (keep running) +``` + +Work in any agent. .md │ │ + Antigravity ┘ (agent updates) └─────────────┘ ▼ + next agent reads handoff +``` + +| Layer | Who runs it | Output | +|-------|-------------|--------| +| **Sync** | `relay watch`, stop hook, .cursor/hooks.json + .cursor/skills/relay-sync/ +├── .claude/settings.json · .codex/hooks.json · .agents/hooks.json +``` + +Registry (all projects): `~/.relay-os/projects.json` + +--- + +## Commands + +| Command | Description | +|---------|-------------| +| `relay init [path]` | Scaffold `.relay/`, hooks, agent patches, API key | +| `relay install [path]` | Re-apply hooks after upgrade | +| `relay serve` | Mission Control (:6374) + API (:3001) | +| `relay watch [path]` | Background sync + compile | +| `relay sync [path]` | Harvest transcripts → `memory.json` | +| `relay compile [path]` | Write `compile_brief.md` | +| `relay context [path]` | Generate `relay_context.md` | +| `relay refresh [path]` | sync + compile + context | +| `relay mcp` | MCP server (stdio) — optional | +| `relay open` | Print UI + API URLs | + +**Pseudo-commands** (patched into agent instructions — not native slash commands): + +| Say | Agent does | +|-----|------------| +| `/relay update` | sync → compile → update IR → `relay context .` | +| `/relay context` | read `.relay/relay_context.md` | +| `/relay init` | run `npx relay-os init` if missing | + +Terminal shortcut: `relay refresh .` ≈ `/relay update` + +--- You type `/relay update`. + +`.relay/current_task.md`: +```markdown +## Now +- Hero done (gradient + CTA) +- Next: projects grid +``` + +**Day 2 — Claude Code** opens the same folder. `/relay context` → implements grid without re-briefing. + +**Day 3 — Copilot CLI** fixes form validation, appends to `.relay/failures.md`. + +With `relay serve` + `relay watch .` running, Mission Control shows all three agents on one timeline. + +**Switch checklist:** `/relay update` → open same folder in new tool → `/relay context` + +--- + +## Dependencies + +| | | +|---|---| +| **Required** | Node.js 18+, npm | +| **Auto-installed** | `express`, `cors` (API) · `next`, `react` (Mission Control) | +| **Not needed** | MongoDB, Redis, Docker, login/OAuth | +| **Optional** | [`sqlite3` CLI](https://sqlite.org/download.html) on PATH — richer Copilot sync via VS Code `state.vscdb` (not an npm package) | +| **Optional LLM keys** | `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` for smarter `relay compile-ir` (heuristics work without) | + +**Storage:** `.relay/` in your project + `~/.relay-os/projects.json` — files only, no DB server. + +**Env vars:** `RELAY_PORT` (3001) · `RELAY_UI_PORT` (6374) · `RELAY_API_KEY` (optional API auth) · `RELAY_SKIP_UI_INSTALL=1` + +--- + +## Stop hooks + +Installed in your **project folder** by `relay init`: + +| Agent | Config | +|-------|--------| +| Cursor | `.cursor/hooks.json` | +| Claude Code | `.claude/settings.json` | +| Codex | `.codex/hooks.json` | +| Copilot CLI | `.github/hooks/relay-os.json` | +| Antigravity | `.agents/hooks.json` | + +Disable: `.relay/config.json` → `"autoAgentUpdate": false` + +--- + +## Docs + +[docs/QUICKSTART.md](docs/QUICKSTART.md) + +--- + +

how to install to npx or npm from github repo

From the GitHub repo **`AspiringPianist/OrbitOS`** (npm package name: **`relay-os`**):

### Run once with npx (no global install)

```bash
cd your-project
npx github:AspiringPianist/OrbitOS init
npx github:AspiringPianist/OrbitOS serve
npx github:AspiringPianist/OrbitOS watch .
```

Pin a branch, tag, or commit:

```bash
npx github:AspiringPianist/OrbitOS#main init
npx github:AspiringPianist/OrbitOS#v0.1.0 init
npx github:AspiringPianist/OrbitOS#abc1234 init
```

### Install globally from GitHub

```bash
npm install -g git+https://github.com/AspiringPianist/OrbitOS.git
relay init
relay serve
```

With branch/tag:

```bash
npm install -g git+https://github.com/AspiringPianist/OrbitOS.git#main
```

### Install in a project (dev dependency)

```bash
npm install --save-dev git+https://github.com/AspiringPianist/OrbitOS.git
npx relay init
```

### Local development (while hacking on the repo)

```bash
git clone https://github.com/AspiringPianist/OrbitOS.git
cd OrbitOS
npm install
npm link

cd /path/to/your-project
relay init
```

**Requires Node.js 18+.** First `relay serve` runs Mission Control’s `npm install` automatically.

Once published to npm, you’d use `npx relay-os init` instead of the `github:` form — same commands, shorter URL.

timeline.content:
Here’s what changed and the honest answer on agent chat from the UI.

## Implemented

**`relay init` now starts Mission Control in the background** (API + UI). First run may take ~30s while deps install. Skip with:
```bash
relay init --no-serve
```

**Relay brain always visible** — right sidebar **RELAY BRAIN** panel shows Handoff, Current task, Decisions, Failures, Project (accordion). **All IR files** tab in the center still has the full split view.

**Collaborators** — sidebar panel; names saved to `.relay/mission_control.json`.

**Agent chat tab** — pick Cursor / Claude / Copilot / Codex / Antigravity, post team notes, get launch instructions for that agent. Stored in the same `mission_control.json`.

## Can you chat with coding agents from Mission Control without opening the IDE?

**Not in the full sense — and we did not wire that up.**

| Agent | From browser only? |
|-------|-------------------|
| **Cursor, Copilot (VS Code), Antigravity** | No — they need their IDE/editor open with the project folder |
| **Claude Code, Codex, Copilot CLI** | Partially — you could shell out to their CLIs from a backend, but that’s a separate integration (terminal sessions, auth, streaming). Relay doesn’t do that today |

What Mission Control **does** do: sync memory, show IR files, coordinate collaborators, and route “use Cursor for this” with `/relay context` instructions. The **Agent chat** tab is for **team notes + which agent to use**, not a replacement for the agent itself.

To actually code with an agent: open the project in that tool (or its CLI), then `/relay context`.

## After pulling these changes

```bash
relay init          # starts UI in background → http://localhost:6374
# or
relay serve         # foreground if you prefer
```

Add your workspace in the UI if it isn’t auto-linked, then check **RELAY BRAIN** on the right and **Agent chat** in the center tabs.

timeline.content:
All agent-facing instructions now use the `relay` npm binary (`relay init`, `relay sync`, `relay context .`, etc.) instead of `npx relay-os`.

### What changed

**Source templates** (used by `relay init` / `relay install` on any project):
- `backend/lib/relayInstall.js` — bootstrap, patched instruction blocks, Cursor rule
- `backend/lib/relayContext.js` — compile brief + context footer text
- `backend/hooks/relay-hook-lib.js` — stop-hook follow-up + fallback invocation
- `bin/relay.js` — help examples and error messages
- `skills/relay-sync/*` — skill source copied into projects

**This workspace** (already installed):
- `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.cursorrules`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`
- `.cursor/rules/relay.mdc`, `.cursor/skills/relay-sync/*`
- `.relay/AGENT_BOOTSTRAP.md`, `.relay/hooks/relay-hook-lib.js`, `RELAY.md`

**Fallback rule** (when `relay` isn’t on PATH):
- Old: `Use npx relay-os if relay is not on PATH`
- New: `Use npx relay if the relay command is not on PATH`

### Agent command reference

| Action | Command |
|--------|---------|
| Install | `relay init` |
| Sync | `relay sync .` |
| Compile | `relay compile .` |
| Handoff | `relay context .` |
| Background | `relay watch .` |
| UI | `relay serve` |

Ran `relay install .` to refresh hooks and bootstrap, and regenerated `compile_brief.md` / `relay_context.md`.

**Note:** README MCP JSON examples still use `"args": ["-y", "relay-os", "mcp"]` for one-off `npx` installs without a local dependency. Say if you want those switched to `"relay", "mcp"` as well.
